mitre_det_name: Detect Abuse of Inter-Process Communication (T1559)
mitre_an_desc: Detects anomalous use of COM, DDE, or named pipes for execution. Correlates creation or access of IPC mechanisms (e.g., named pipes, COM objects) with unusual parent-child process relationships or code injection patterns (e.g., Office spawning cmd.exe via DDE).

mitre_det_name: Detect Abuse of Inter-Process Communication (T1559)
mitre_an_desc: Detects anomalous use of Mach ports, Apple Events, or XPC services for inter-process execution or code injection. Focuses on unexpected processes attempting to send privileged Apple Events (e.g., automation scripts injecting into security-sensitive apps).

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Unusual Mach port registration or access attempts between unrelated processes

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:osquery
mitre_log_sources.channel: exec: Unexpected execution of osascript or AppleScript targeting sensitive apps

mitre_det_name: Detect Abuse of Inter-Process Communication (T1559)
mitre_an_desc: Detects abuse of UNIX domain sockets, pipes, or message queues for unauthorized code execution. Correlates unexpected socket creation with suspicious binaries, abnormal shell pipelines, or injected processes establishing IPC channels.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: socket: Suspicious creation of AF_UNIX sockets outside expected daemons

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: open: Access to named pipes or FIFO in /tmp or /dev/shm by unexpected processes

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Encrypted Channel across OS Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Unusual TLS tunnels through ports not normally encrypted (e.g., TLS on port 8080, 53). Defender sees NetFlow/IPFIX or packet inspection indicating high-entropy traffic volumes and asymmetric client/server exchange ratios.

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: Session records with TLS-like byte patterns

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Connections
mitre_log_sources.channel: Abnormal certificate chains or non-standard ports carrying TLS

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Encrypted Channel across OS Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Applications or launchd jobs initiating encrypted TLS traffic to rare external hosts. Defender observes unified logs showing ssl/TLS API calls by processes not baseline-approved, and payload entropy suggesting encrypted C2 sessions.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Encrypted session initiation by unexpected binary

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Process invoking SSL routines from Security framework

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Encrypted Channel across OS Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Processes that normally do not initiate network connections establishing outbound encrypted TLS/SSL sessions, especially with asymmetric traffic volumes (client sending more than receiving) or non-standard certificate chains. Defender observations correlate process creation with unexpected network encryption libraries being loaded.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Encrypted Channel across OS Platforms
mitre_an_desc: VMware management daemons or guest processes initiating encrypted connections outside expected vCenter, update servers, or internal comms. Defender identifies hostd or vpxa initiating outbound TLS flows with uncommon destinations.

mitre_log_sources.name: esxi:vpxd
mitre_log_sources.channel: TLS session established by ESXi service to unapproved endpoint

mitre_log_sources.name: esxi:vmkernel
mitre_log_sources.channel: Inspection of sockets showing encrypted sessions from non-baseline processes

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Encrypted Channel across OS Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Processes like curl, wget, python, socat, or custom binaries initiating TLS/SSL sessions to non-standard destinations. Defender sees abnormal syscalls for connect(), loading of libssl libraries, and persistent outbound encrypted traffic from daemons not normally communicating externally.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: socket/connect with TLS context by unexpected process

mitre_log_sources.name: linux:syslog
mitre_log_sources.channel: system daemons initiating TLS sessions outside expected services

mitre_log_sources.name: linux:osquery
mitre_log_sources.channel: Processes linked with libssl or crypto libraries making outbound connections

mitre_det_name: Detection of Command and Control Over Application Layer Protocols
mitre_an_desc: Detects suspicious usage of common application-layer protocols (e.g., HTTP, HTTPS, DNS, SMB) by abnormal processes, with high outbound byte counts or irregular ports, possibly indicating command and control or data exfiltration.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Command and Control Over Application Layer Protocols
mitre_an_desc: Detects suspicious curl, wget, or custom socket traffic that leverages DNS, HTTPS, or IRC-style protocols with unbalanced traffic or beacon-like intervals.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Command and Control Over Application Layer Protocols
mitre_an_desc: Detects applications using abnormal protocols or high volume traffic not previously associated with the process image, such as Automator or AppleScript invoking curl or python sockets.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Command and Control Over Application Layer Protocols
mitre_an_desc: Detects application-layer tunneling or unauthorized app protocols like DNS-over-HTTPS, embedded C2 in TLS/HTTP headers, or misused SMB traffic crossing VLANs.

mitre_det_name: Detection of Local Browser Artifact Access for Reconnaissance
mitre_an_desc: Scripting or CLI tool access to ~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome or ~/Library/Safari bookmarks, cookies, or history databases. Detection relies on unexpected processes accessing or reading from these locations.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Access to ~/Library/*/Safari or Chrome directories by non-browser processes

mitre_det_name: Detection of Local Browser Artifact Access for Reconnaissance
mitre_an_desc: Unauthorized shell or script-based access to browser config or SQLite history files, typically in ~/.config/google-chrome/, ~/.mozilla/, or ~/.var/app folders, indicating enumeration of bookmarks or saved credentials.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: open, read, or stat of browser config files

mitre_log_sources.name: linux:syslog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Suspicious script or command execution targeting browser folders

mitre_det_name: Detection of Local Browser Artifact Access for Reconnaissance
mitre_an_desc: Access to browser artifact locations (e.g., Chrome, Edge, Firefox) by processes like PowerShell, cmd.exe, or unknown tools, followed by file reads, decoding, or export operations indicating enumeration of bookmarks, autofill, or history databases.
sigma_rule_title: Automated Collection Bookmarks Using Get-ChildItem PowerShell

sigma_rule_log_source.definition: Requirements: Script Block Logging must be enabled

mitre_det_name: Detection of Local Browser Artifact Access for Reconnaissance
mitre_an_desc: Access to browser artifact locations (e.g., Chrome, Edge, Firefox) by processes like PowerShell, cmd.exe, or unknown tools, followed by file reads, decoding, or export operations indicating enumeration of bookmarks, autofill, or history databases.

mitre_det_name: Drive-by Compromise — Behavior-based, Multi-platform Detection Strategy (T1189)
mitre_an_desc: Correlated evidence where Safari/Chrome/WebKit-based processes issue network requests for uncommon or obfuscated JS resources followed by spawning of script interpreters, launchd or ad-hoc binaries, unusual child processes, or dynamic library loads into browser processes. Defender sees: proxy/HTTP logs with suspicious resource content + unifiedlogs/ASL showing browser/plugin crashes or extension loads + process events indicating child process creation and file writes to /var/folders or /tmp shortly after the fetch.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Logs from unifiedlogging that show browser crashes, plugin enumerations, extension installs or errors around the same time as suspicious network fetches

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: process_create: Process creation where parent is Safari/Google Chrome and child is script interpreter or signed-but-unusual helper binary

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: New files written to /var/folders, /tmp, ~/Library/Caches, or ~/Downloads by browser context or its children

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: HTTP/HTTPS requests for script resources flagged by content inspection (excessive obfuscation, eval usage, unusual redirects)

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Anomalous dyld dynamic library loads or RWX memory mappings in browser process

mitre_det_name: Drive-by Compromise — Behavior-based, Multi-platform Detection Strategy (T1189)
mitre_an_desc: Correlated evidence where Safari/Chrome/WebKit-based processes issue network requests for uncommon or obfuscated JS resources followed by spawning of script interpreters, launchd or ad-hoc binaries, unusual child processes, or dynamic library loads into browser processes. Defender sees: proxy/HTTP logs with suspicious resource content + unifiedlogs/ASL showing browser/plugin crashes or extension loads + process events indicating child process creation and file writes to /var/folders or /tmp shortly after the fetch.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Logs from unifiedlogging that show browser crashes, plugin enumerations, extension installs or errors around the same time as suspicious network fetches

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: process_create: Process creation where parent is Safari/Google Chrome and child is script interpreter or signed-but-unusual helper binary

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: New files written to /var/folders, /tmp, ~/Library/Caches, or ~/Downloads by browser context or its children

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: HTTP/HTTPS requests for script resources flagged by content inspection (excessive obfuscation, eval usage, unusual redirects)

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Anomalous dyld dynamic library loads or RWX memory mappings in browser process

mitre_det_name: Drive-by Compromise — Behavior-based, Multi-platform Detection Strategy (T1189)
mitre_an_desc: Post-compromise identity & session anomalies that follow a drive-by compromise: token reuse from new/unfamiliar IPs, anomalous sign-in patterns for previously inactive users, unexpected consent/grant events, or provisioning changes. Defender sees an endpoint/browser compromise (network + endpoint signals) followed by unusual IdP events: new refresh token issuance, consent/consent-grant events, odd MFA bypass patterns, or unusual OAuth client registrations.

mitre_log_sources.name: azure:signinlogs
mitre_log_sources.channel: SignIn: Sign-ins flagged as atypical (new geographic region, unfamiliar device id) shortly after correlated endpoint/browser compromise times

mitre_log_sources.name: m365:unified
mitre_log_sources.channel: Application Consent grants, new OAuth client registrations, or unusual admin-level activities executed by a user account shortly after suspected drive-by compromise

mitre_log_sources.name: saas:auth
mitre_log_sources.channel: Refresh token issuance or refresh token usage from new IPs or user agents

mitre_log_sources.name: AWS:CloudTrail
mitre_log_sources.channel: ConsoleLogin: If IdP backed by cloud provider, Console login from new IP/agent after correlated endpoint compromise

mitre_det_name: Drive-by Compromise — Behavior-based, Multi-platform Detection Strategy (T1189)
mitre_an_desc: Correlated evidence of browser or webview fetches to uncommon domains or mutated JS resources (proxy/NGFW logs + Zeek/HTTP logs) followed by unexpected interpreters or script engines executing (python, ruby, sh) spawned from browser processes or user sessions, rapid on-disk staging in /tmp, and outbound connections that deviate from baseline. Defender sees: uncommon resource fetch → short-lived child process executions from user browser context → file writes in temp directories → anomalous outbound C2-like connections.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: execve: execve calls where a browser/webview process is parent and child is interpreter (python, sh, ruby) or downloader (curl, wget)

mitre_log_sources.name: linux:syslog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Application or browser logs (webview errors, plugin enumerations) indicating suspicious script evaluation or plugin loads

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: http::response: HTTP responses with suspicious content-type for scripts, long obfuscated javascript bodies, or redirects to exploit kit domains

mitre_log_sources.name: linux:Sysmon
mitre_log_sources.channel: New files in /tmp, /var/tmp, $HOME/.cache, executed within TimeWindow after browser HTTP fetch

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Connections
mitre_log_sources.channel: Outbound connections from newly spawned child processes or from the browser to uncommon endpoints or on anomalous ports

mitre_det_name: Drive-by Compromise — Behavior-based, Multi-platform Detection Strategy (T1189)
mitre_an_desc: Correlated evidence of anomalous browser/network behavior (suspicious external resource fetches and script injection patterns) followed by atypical child processes, ephemeral execution contexts, memory modification or process injection, and unexpected file drops. Defender sees network requests to previously unseen/suspicious domains or resources + browser process spawning unusual children or loading unsigned modules + file writes or registry changes shortly after those requests.

mitre_log_sources.name: WinEventLog:Application
mitre_log_sources.channel: Browser or plugin/application logs showing script errors, plugin enumerations, or unusual extension load events

mitre_log_sources.name: etw:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-Process
mitre_log_sources.channel: Memory Modification / Unmapped module load or suspicious RWX allocations in the process space of a browser process

mitre_log_sources.name: NSM:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: http.request: HTTP requests and responses for specific script resources, unexpected content-types (application/octet-stream for script URLs), suspicious referrers, or obfuscated javascript resources

mitre_det_name: Multi-Platform Detection Strategy for T1678 - Delay Execution
mitre_an_desc: Correlated use of sleep/delay mechanisms (e.g., kernel32!Sleep, NTDLL APIs) in short-lived processes, combined with parent processes invoking suspicious scripts (e.g., wscript, powershell) with minimal user interaction.

mitre_det_name: Multi-Platform Detection Strategy for T1678 - Delay Execution
mitre_an_desc: Execution of AppleScript, bash, or launchd jobs that invoke delay functions (e.g., sleep, delay in AppleScript) with limited parent interaction and staged follow-on commands.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: launchd or osascript spawns process with delay command

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: delay/sleep library usage in user context

mitre_det_name: Multi-Platform Detection Strategy for T1678 - Delay Execution
mitre_an_desc: Shell scripts or binaries invoking repeated 'sleep', 'ping', or low-level syscalls (e.g., nanosleep) in short-lived execution chains with no user or system interaction. Frequently seen in malicious cron jobs or payload stagers.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: execve or nanosleep with no stdout/stderr I/O

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Hijack Execution Flow across OS platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Unusual modifications to service binary paths, registry keys, or DLL load paths resulting in alternate execution flow. Defender observes registry key modifications, suspicious file writes into system directories, and processes loading libraries from abnormal paths.
sigma_rule_title: Potential Initial Access via DLL Search Order Hijacking

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Hijack Execution Flow across OS platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Unusual modifications to service binary paths, registry keys, or DLL load paths resulting in alternate execution flow. Defender observes registry key modifications, suspicious file writes into system directories, and processes loading libraries from abnormal paths.
sigma_rule_title: Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Uncommon Extension

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Hijack Execution Flow across OS platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Unusual modifications to service binary paths, registry keys, or DLL load paths resulting in alternate execution flow. Defender observes registry key modifications, suspicious file writes into system directories, and processes loading libraries from abnormal paths.

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Hijack Execution Flow across OS platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Adversary manipulation of shared library paths, environment variables, or replacement of service binaries. Defender observes suspicious modifications in /etc/ld.so.preload, service config changes, or file writes replacing existing executables.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: open/write syscalls targeting /etc/ld.so.preload or binaries in /usr/bin

mitre_log_sources.name: linux:syslog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Service restart with modified executable path

mitre_log_sources.name: linux:osquery
mitre_log_sources.channel: Process execution with LD_PRELOAD or modified library path

mitre_det_name: Detection Strategy for Hijack Execution Flow across OS platforms.
mitre_an_desc: Abuse of DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES or hijacking framework paths for malicious libraries. Defender observes processes invoking abnormal dylibs, modified plist files, or persistence entries pointing to altered binaries.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Execution of process with DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES set

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Modified application plist or binary replacement in /Applications

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Process Injection Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects process injection by correlating memory manipulation API calls (e.g., VirtualAllocEx, WriteProcessMemory), suspicious thread creation (e.g., CreateRemoteThread), and unusual DLL loads within another process's context.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Process Injection Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects process injection by correlating memory manipulation API calls (e.g., VirtualAllocEx, WriteProcessMemory), suspicious thread creation (e.g., CreateRemoteThread), and unusual DLL loads within another process's context.

mitre_an_desc: Detects process injection by correlating memory manipulation API calls (e.g., VirtualAllocEx, WriteProcessMemory), suspicious thread creation (e.g., CreateRemoteThread), and unusual DLL loads within another process's context.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Process Injection Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects memory-based injection by monitoring `task_for_pid`, `mach_vm_write`, and dylib injection patterns through `DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES` or manual memory mapping.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Process Injection Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects ptrace- or memfd-based process injection through audit logs capturing system calls (e.g., ptrace, mmap) targeting running processes along with suspicious file descriptors or memory writes.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Event Triggered Execution Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Monitors cloud function creation triggered by specific audit log events (e.g., IAM changes, object creation), followed by anomalous behavior from new service accounts.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Event Triggered Execution Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Correlates launchd plist modifications with subsequent unauthorized script execution or anomalous parent-child process trees involving user agents.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Execution of launchctl with suspicious arguments

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Event Triggered Execution Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects inotify or auditd configuration changes that monitor system files coupled with execution of script interpreters or binaries by cron or systemd timers.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: Inotify watch creation or auditctl changes on /etc/cron* or /lib/systemd/system/

mitre_log_sources.name: linux:syslog
mitre_log_sources.channel: Execution of non-standard script or binary by cron

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: Execution of script interpreters by systemd timer (ExecStart)

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Event Triggered Execution Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Correlates unexpected modifications to WMI event filters, scheduled task triggers, or registry autorun keys with subsequent execution of non-standard binaries by SYSTEM-level processes.

mitre_log_sources.name: WinEventLog:WMI
mitre_log_sources.channel: Creation or modification of __EventFilter, __FilterToConsumerBinding, or CommandLineEventConsumer

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Event Triggered Execution Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Correlates Power Automate or similar logic app workflows triggered by SaaS file uploads or email rules with data forwarding or anomalous access patterns.

mitre_log_sources.name: m365:unified
mitre_log_sources.channel: Creation of Power Automate flow triggered by OneDrive or Exchange event

mitre_log_sources.name: m365:unified
mitre_log_sources.channel: Automated forwarding or file sync initiated by a logic app

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Event Triggered Execution Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects macros or VBA triggers set to execute on document open or close events, often correlating with embedded payloads or C2 traffic shortly after execution.

mitre_log_sources.name: m365:office
mitre_log_sources.channel: VBA auto_open, auto_close, or document_open events

mitre_log_sources.name: m365:office
mitre_log_sources.channel: External HTTP/DNS connection from Office binary shortly after macro trigger

mitre_det_name: Exploitation for Client Execution – cross-platform behavior chain (browser/Office/3rd-party apps)
mitre_an_desc: Cause→effect chain: (1) A client app (browser, Office, PDF/Flash/reader) experiences a crash/abnormal exit or loads from an unusual location, then (2) drops or modifies a file in user-writable paths, and/or (3) spawns an unexpected child (e.g., powershell/cmd/mshta/rundll32/wscript/installer), and (4) establishes outbound C2-like connections shortly after. Correlate application logs, file writes, process lineage, and network egress within a short window.

mitre_det_name: Exploitation for Client Execution – cross-platform behavior chain (browser/Office/3rd-party apps)
mitre_an_desc: Cause→effect chain: (1) App crash/abnormal termination in unified logs for Safari/Chrome/Office/Preview, (2) new files/scripts in ~/Library, ~/Downloads, /private/var/folders/*, (3) unexpected child (osascript, zsh, bash, curl) spawned by those apps, (4) new outbound connections.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: process crash, abort, code signing violations

mitre_det_name: Exploitation for Client Execution – cross-platform behavior chain (browser/Office/3rd-party apps)
mitre_an_desc: Cause→effect chain: (1) App crash/abnormal termination in unified logs for Safari/Chrome/Office/Preview, (2) new files/scripts in ~/Library, ~/Downloads, /private/var/folders/*, (3) unexpected child (osascript, zsh, bash, curl) spawned by those apps, (4) new outbound connections.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: process crash, abort, code signing violations

mitre_det_name: Exploitation for Client Execution – cross-platform behavior chain (browser/Office/3rd-party apps)
mitre_an_desc: Cause→effect chain: (1) Browser/Office/reader process logs crash/segfault or abnormal sandbox message, (2) new executable/script/write occurs in $HOME (Downloads, ~/.cache, /tmp), (3) unexpected child like curl/wget/bash/python opens network connections soon after.

mitre_log_sources.name: NetFlow:Flow
mitre_log_sources.channel: new outbound connections from exploited process tree

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Obfuscated Files or Information
mitre_an_desc: Monitors use of archive or encryption tools (zip, openssl) tied to user-scripted activity or binaries writing encoded payloads under /Users or /Volumes.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Obfuscated Files or Information
mitre_an_desc: Correlates script execution or suspicious parent processes with creation or modification of encoded, compressed, or encrypted file formats (e.g., .zip, .7z, .enc) and abnormal command-line syntax or PowerShell obfuscation.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Obfuscated Files or Information
mitre_an_desc: Correlates script execution or suspicious parent processes with creation or modification of encoded, compressed, or encrypted file formats (e.g., .zip, .7z, .enc) and abnormal command-line syntax or PowerShell obfuscation.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Obfuscated Files or Information
mitre_an_desc: Correlates script execution or suspicious parent processes with creation or modification of encoded, compressed, or encrypted file formats (e.g., .zip, .7z, .enc) and abnormal command-line syntax or PowerShell obfuscation.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Obfuscated Files or Information
mitre_an_desc: Correlates script execution or suspicious parent processes with creation or modification of encoded, compressed, or encrypted file formats (e.g., .zip, .7z, .enc) and abnormal command-line syntax or PowerShell obfuscation.

sigma_rule_title: Potential CommandLine Obfuscation Using Unicode Characters

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Obfuscated Files or Information
mitre_an_desc: Detects use of gzip, base64, tar, or openssl in scripts or commands that encode/encrypt files after file staging or system enumeration.
sigma_rule_title: Suspicious Filename with Embedded Base64 Commands

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Obfuscated Files or Information
mitre_an_desc: Identifies transfer of base64, uuencoded, or high-entropy files over HTTP, FTP, or custom protocols in lateral movement or exfiltration streams.

mitre_det_name: Behavioral Detection of Obfuscated Files or Information
mitre_an_desc: Detects encoded PowerCLI or Base64-encoded payloads staged via datastore uploads or shell access (e.g., ESXi Shell or backdoored VIBs).

mitre_log_sources.name: esxi:hostd
mitre_log_sources.channel: Remote access API calls and file uploads

mitre_det_name: Detection of System Process Creation or Modification Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects creation of new container system processes via `docker run --restart`, `kubectl exec` to init containers, or modification of container init specs. Flags container images that override entrypoints to embed persistence behaviors.

mitre_log_sources.name: docker:events
mitre_log_sources.channel: docker run with restart=always or modifying init

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: modification of entrypoint scripts or init containers

mitre_det_name: Detection of System Process Creation or Modification Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects command-line or API-based creation/modification of Windows Services via `sc.exe`, `powershell.exe`, `services.exe`, or `ChangeServiceConfig`. Looks for creation/modification of autostart services via registry changes, file drops to `System32\services`, and anomalous parent-child process trees.

mitre_det_name: Detection of System Process Creation or Modification Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects creation or modification of `systemd` service units, addition of cron jobs that invoke binaries on boot, or suspicious writes to `/etc/init.d/`. Monitors `chmod +x` and `systemctl` execution paths, especially from non-root parent processes.

mitre_log_sources.name: auditd:SYSCALL
mitre_log_sources.channel: execution of systemctl or service with enable/start/modify

mitre_det_name: Detection of System Process Creation or Modification Across Platforms
mitre_an_desc: Detects creation or modification of `LaunchDaemon` or `LaunchAgent` plist files under `/Library/LaunchDaemons/`, `~/Library/LaunchAgents/`, or similar. Monitors execution of `launchctl`, property list edits, and file permission changes.

mitre_log_sources.name: macos:unifiedlog
mitre_log_sources.channel: launchctl load/unload or plist file modification

mitre_log_sources.name: fs:fsusage
mitre_log_sources.channel: file write to launchd plist paths
